questions.text: Why does the Surah begin with oaths about angels and unseen events?
questions.answer: To establish the reality of the unseen and prepare the listener for the certainty of resurrection.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that the unseen world has no connection to human accountability.
questions.answer: False. It links the unseen to resurrection, judgment, and moral lessons.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the suddenness of resurrection?
questions.answer: It describes it as happening with a single shout, showing Allah’s power and speed of execution.

questions.text: How does the Surah respond to mockery about decayed bones?
questions.answer: It shows resurrection is easy for Allah; denial is ignorance and arrogance.

questions.text: What is one emotional description of the Day in this Surah?
questions.answer: Hearts will be trembling with terror, showing the seriousness of the moment.

questions.text: Why does the Surah include the story of Musa and Pharaoh?
questions.answer: To show the pattern of tyranny, the call to purification, and Allah’s punishment of arrogance.

questions.text: What was the core invitation Musa gave to Pharaoh according to this Surah?
questions.answer: To purify himself and fear Allah—moral reform, not merely political change.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that tyrants fall because they lack intelligence, not because of arrogance.
questions.answer: False. It emphasizes arrogance and transgression as the root.

questions.text: What does Pharaoh’s claim 'I am your most exalted lord' teach about shirk?
questions.answer: Shirk can include human arrogance that demands worship-like obedience and absolute authority.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach that Allah’s punishment can be an example for others?
questions.answer: Pharaoh’s end is presented as a lesson and warning for those who fear Allah.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach by mentioning the sky’s construction and the earth’s spreading?
questions.answer: Creation’s magnitude is proof Allah can resurrect and that life has a Creator and purpose.

questions.text: What is one lesson from Allah bringing forth water and pasture?
questions.answer: Provision is from Allah; gratitude and obedience are the correct response.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that nature runs on its own without Allah.
questions.answer: False. It attributes creation and provision directly to Allah.

questions.text: What does 'At-Tammat al-Kubra' represent emotionally?
questions.answer: An overwhelming calamity that dwarfs all worldly worries and exposes true priorities.

questions.text: What will a person remember on that Day according to this Surah?
questions.answer: What they strove for—the reality of deeds, intentions, and effort becomes clear.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the two paths of people regarding desires?
questions.answer: Some prefer dunya and transgress, others fear standing before Allah and restrain desires.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that restraining desires is unnecessary if someone believes in their heart.
questions.answer: False. It links restraining desires to Paradise.

questions.text: What is one practical meaning of restraining the soul from desires?
questions.answer: Avoiding haram, controlling anger and lust, and choosing Allah’s pleasure over impulse.

questions.text: Why does the Surah say knowledge of the Hour belongs only to Allah?
questions.answer: To stop speculation and push people toward preparation rather than curiosity.

questions.text: Why does this Surah limit the Prophet’s knowledge about the exact time of the Hour?
questions.answer: Because the exact time is with Allah alone; the Prophet’s role is to warn and remind, not to control or know the unseen time of the Hour independently.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach that dunya feels short in hindsight?
questions.answer: On that Day, worldly life seems like only a morning or afternoon compared to eternity.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that long life in dunya guarantees long-term satisfaction.
questions.answer: False. Dunya feels brief when the Hereafter is seen.

questions.text: How can this Surah help someone break arrogance?
questions.answer: By showing Pharaoh’s end and reminding that creation and judgment belong to Allah alone.

questions.text: How can a modern reader apply this Surah today?
questions.answer: Stop speculating about timelines; focus on repentance, prayer, and controlling desires.

questions.text: What is one concluding takeaway from Surah An-Nazi'at?
questions.answer: Tyranny and desire end in Hell; fear of Allah and self-restraint end in Paradise.

questions.text: The angels who extract the souls of the disbelievers violently.
questions.answer: The angels who extract the souls of the disbelievers violently.

questions.text: Who are 'An-Nashitat' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: The angels who draw out the souls of the believers gently.

questions.text: What will happen during the 'first blast' (ar-Rajifah) and the 'subsequent one' (ar-Radifah)?
questions.answer: The first signals death/destruction, and the second signals the resurrection.

questions.text: What will be the state of hearts on that Day?
questions.answer: Throbbing/palpitating with terror.

questions.text: What is the disbelievers' mocking question about bones?
questions.answer: 'Even after we are decayed bones, [will we be resurrected]?'.

questions.text: How many 'shouts' will it take for everyone to be on the surface of the earth?
questions.answer: A single shout (Zajrah).

questions.text: Where did Allah call Musa (AS) in the story mentioned?
questions.answer: In the sacred valley of Tuwa.

questions.text: What was the mission given to Musa (AS) regarding Pharaoh?
questions.answer: To go to Pharaoh, for he had transgressed, and ask if he would purify himself.

questions.text: What was 'the great sign' Musa showed Pharaoh?
questions.answer: His staff turning into a serpent (or his white hand).

questions.text: What was Pharaoh's ultimate claim of arrogance?
questions.answer: He said: 'I am your most exalted lord'.

questions.text: How did Allah punish Pharaoh?
questions.answer: He seized him with an exemplary punishment for the last and the first [transgression].

questions.text: What is a sign of Allah's power in the construction of the sky?
questions.answer: He raised its ceiling and proportioned it, darkened its night, and brought out its bright morning.

questions.text: What does this Surah mention Allah did with the earth after the heaven?
questions.answer: He spread it out, brought forth its water and pasture, and made the mountains firm.

questions.text: What is 'At-Tammat al-Kubra'?
questions.answer: The Greatest Overwhelming Calamity (a name for the Day of Judgment).

questions.text: What will man remember on that Day?
questions.answer: Whatever he strove for in the world.

questions.text: Where will be the refuge for the one who transgressed and preferred the life of the world?
questions.answer: Hellfire.

questions.text: Where will be the refuge for the one who feared the standing before his Lord and restrained his soul from desire?
questions.answer: Paradise.

questions.text: Do people ask the Prophet about the 'Hour'?
questions.answer: Yes, but its knowledge is only with Allah.

questions.text: What is the Prophet's role regarding the Hour?
questions.answer: He is only a warner for those who fear it.

questions.text: How long will the world seem to people when they see the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Like an afternoon or a morning.
